title: Establishment of axenic sea-ice diatom cultures, modified from Jaeckisch et al. (2011)

steps: Pre-treat an optically dense diatom culture with 3 min of vortex-mixing (< 8°C) and 30 sec of ultrasonication (80% in 5-s pulses) on ice to remove attached bacteria from diatoms and EPS aggregates.
Centrifuge cells at 1000 × g for 10 min (< 8°C) and wash the pellet twice with sterile F/2 medium (Guillard, 1975) to remove the majority of loose bacterial cells.
Add six antibiotics to the medium according to Table 1 (see abstract), and incubate for 5 days at –1°C and under light (20–45 µmol photons m -2 s -1 ).
Centrifuge cells at 1000 × g for 10 min (< 8°C) wash pellet in fresh F/2 medium.
Pick < 10 cells to start the axenic cultures (pipetting or plate on F/2 agar to pick single colonies).

After recovery and growth (~2 months), check for bacterial cells using DAPI staining and after agar plating on half-strength BD Difco™ Marine Agar 2216 (< 8°C), kept at seawater salinity using artificial seawater (411 mM NaCl, 9.39 mM KCl, 26.1 m M MgCl 2 6H 2 O, 28.4 mM MgSO 4 ·7H 2 O, 5.01 mM TAPSO buffer).
If bacteria are still present, repeat protocol. Axenic cultures are usually established within the first or second attempt.
creator: Anders Torstensson
